L1.area: P2P network message validation and transaction processing pipeline

L2.description: A nil-pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the transaction validation pathway of a blockchain node's peer-to-peer message processing. When a specially crafted transaction message with an omitted sub-message field is received from the network, it causes a nil-pointer panic during version checking. Since the validation callback runs in an unrecovered goroutine, the panic crashes the entire node process. An unauthenticated remote peer can trigger this with a minimal payload, potentially halting the chain if enough validators are targeted.

L3.area: Transaction version validation within the P2P gossip topic interceptor chain
L3.description: A nil-pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the transaction version checking logic of the P2P gossip message validation chain. When a protobuf-encoded transaction message arrives with its embedded raw data sub-message omitted, the version checker dereferences the nil field without a guard, causing a panic. The panic propagates through the unrecovered pubsub callback goroutine and crashes the node. Any unauthenticated libp2p peer can send this minimal payload on the transactions gossip topic to deterministically crash any receiving node.

ground_truth.reason: txVersionChecker.CheckTxVersion dereferences tx.RawData.Version without a nil check; a protobuf Transaction with RawData omitted unmarshals to RawData == nil, causing a nil-pointer panic that crashes the node via the unrecovered pubsub callback.
